model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Louisville holds a slight edge in overall efficiency metrics, ranking 16th in Torvik and 18th in KenPom compared to UCLA's 24th and 27th, with a more balanced offensive attack and a defense that has proven elite all season. While UCLA's offense is potent, their defensive rating is weaker (49th in Torvik), and Louisville's recent form—including a 23-10 record and strong ACC play—provides a reliable path to victory. The underdog's path for UCLA relies on hot three-point shooting and forcing turnovers, but Louisville's superior efficiency, depth, and matchup advantage make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate Team Ratings and Matchup Data
reasoningStep.summary: Gathered raw statistics from KenPom and Torvik for both Louisville and UCLA to assess team quality and matchup specifics.
reasoningStep.vidence: Louisville ranks 16th in Torvik (adjO 124.0, adjD 98.1) and 18th in KenPom (adjO 124.1, adjD 98.7). UCLA ranks 24th in Torvik (adjO 124.5, adjD 101.7) and 27th in KenPom (adjO 123.8, adjD 102.1). Louisville has a stronger defensive efficiency and slightly better overall rating.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Scott Van Pelt and Stanford Steve are here to react to the 2026 NCAA Tournament March Madness bracket. Steve gives his UNFILTERED thoughts on all the issues with the bracket. Duke's draw is TOUGH, and Steve is giving his Blue Devils NO CHANCE of reaching Indy. A potential Florida-Vandy rematch isn't ideal, and a road game in the Elite 8?? Plus, will chalk indeed fly? Is it Arizona's year? Which upsets does SVP like the most? Plus, a solution to the flopping problem, reaction to Bam Adebayo's 83-point game and an in-depth breakdown of Cam Young's Players Championship victory. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: s16-east-2
Current game label: East Sweet 16 2
Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Louisville (louisville) vs UCLA (ucla)
Prior committed picks: 53
